items.title: Tehran and Washington sign interim peace deal to end US-Israeli war on Iran
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed an interim deal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran. The two countries now have 60 days to hammer out a lasting agreement that would see Tehran diluting its enriched uranium stocks in exchange for sweeping sanctions relief and a $300 billion reconstruction fund.
items.content: President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed an interim deal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran. The two countries now have 60 days to hammer out a lasting agreement that would see Tehran diluting its enriched uranium stocks in exchange for sweeping sanctions relief and a $300 billion reconstruction fund.

items.title: Trump formally signs U.S.-Iran deal as questions linger about nuclear program
items.standfirst: President Trump warned he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave." The U.S. and Iran signed the memo of understanding remotely, a White House official said.
items.content: President Trump warned he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave." The U.S. and Iran signed the memo of understanding remotely, a White House official said.

items.title: G7 hails unity on Russia as Trump signals tougher line on Moscow
items.standfirst: G7 leaders welcomed growing unity on Wednesday in efforts to increase pressure on Russia to end the war in Ukraine, amid signs US President Donald Trump is taking a tougher line on Moscow. Later, at the candlelit dinner in Versailles, Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran aimed at ending the conflict in the Middle East.
items.content: G7 leaders welcomed growing unity on Wednesday in efforts to increase pressure on Russia to end the war in Ukraine, amid signs US President Donald Trump is taking a tougher line on Moscow. Later, at the candlelit dinner in Versailles, Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran aimed at ending the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Breaking down the preliminary U.S.-Iran deal as Trump, Iranian president sign remotely
items.standfirst: A White House official tells CBS News that President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian remotely signed a memorandum of understanding to end the Iran war. Eric Lob, associate professor of politics and international relations at Florida International University, joins with analysis.

items.title: Middle East live: Oil prices fall more than 3% after Trump signs deal reopening Hormuz Strait
items.standfirst: Oil prices fell more than 3% Thursday after US President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian signed off on a deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas exports flowed through the waterway before Tehran effectively closed it off after the war began. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Oil prices fell more than 3% Thursday after US President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian signed off on a deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas exports flowed through the waterway before Tehran effectively closed it off after the war began. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Former Suffolk County police commissioner reacts to Gilgo Beach killer sentencing
items.standfirst: The Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer was sentenced to life in prison without parole on Wednesday. Former Suffolk County Police Commissioner Rodney Harrison, who formed the task force that ultimately led to Rex Heuermann's arrest, joins with analysis.
items.content: The Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer was sentenced to life in prison without parole on Wednesday. Former Suffolk County Police Commissioner Rodney Harrison, who formed the task force that ultimately led to Rex Heuermann's arrest, joins with analysis.

items.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
items.standfirst: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.
items.content: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

items.title: Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life without parole
items.standfirst: New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life without parole for the murders of eight women.
items.content: New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life without parole for the murders of eight women.

items.title: Families of eight women killed confront the Gilgo Beach serial killer: ‘Save a spot in hell’
items.standfirst: New York judge imposes life without parole after emotional statements at Rex Heuermann’s sentencingSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe families of eight women killed by Rex Heuermann spoke at the Gilgo Beach killer’s sentencing in Riverhead, New York, on Wednesday, more than three decades after the 62-year-old Manhattan architect began his killing spree.Heuermann pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted to the killing of an eighth victim in April. Just before being sentenced to life in prison without parole on Wednesday for his admitted crimes, Heuermann offered a weak, generalized apology for his actions. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with 'large-scale' attack, oil refinery strike 'justified', Zelensky says
clusters.neutral_headline: Moscow oil refinery on fire after ‘large-scale’ Ukrainian drone strike

Several drones managed to reach the [Moscow oil refinery],” said Sergei Sobyanin, the city’s mayor, adding that a shopping centre was also damaged

items.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with 'large-scale' attack, oil refinery strike 'justified', Zelensky says
items.standfirst: Smoke rose over Moscow Thursday morning after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack in years, hitting the Moscow oil refinery and sparking fires in and around the capital. The attack on the oil refinery was a "justified response" to Russian strikes in Ukraine, said President Volodymyr Zelensky.
items.content: Smoke rose over Moscow Thursday morning after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack in years, hitting the Moscow oil refinery and sparking fires in and around the capital. The attack on the oil refinery was a "justified response" to Russian strikes in Ukraine, said President Volodymyr Zelensky.

items.title: Moscow oil refinery on fire after ‘large-scale’ Ukrainian drone strike
items.standfirst: Mayor of Russian capital claims ‌about 180 drones heading to the city were shot down as flames and smoke billow over outskirtsUkrainian drones hit Moscow’s ⁠oil refinery for the second time ⁠this week, sending out flames and plumes ⁠of smoke over the south-eastern district of Kapotnya on Thursday morning.“Air defence forces are continuing to repel a large-scale attack. Several drones managed to reach the [Moscow oil refinery],” said Sergei Sobyanin, the city’s mayor, adding that a shopping centre was also damaged. He claimed ‌about 180 drones heading for the capital had ‌been downed. Continue reading...

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur weakens off Texas' Gulf Coast
items.standfirst: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas before later weakening to a post-tropical cyclone.
items.content: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas before later weakening to a post-tropical cyclone.

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur hits Texas coast with floods as Midwest faces tornadoes, strong winds
items.standfirst: Millions of Americans along the Gulf Coast are bracing for more torrential rain from Tropical Storm Arthur that could lead to life-threatening flash flooding. Parts of the Midwest are under a tornado watch and could also experience flooding. CBS News national weather correspondent Rob Maricano has the latest from Illinois.
items.content: Millions of Americans along the Gulf Coast are bracing for more torrential rain from Tropical Storm Arthur that could lead to life-threatening flash flooding. Parts of the Midwest are under a tornado watch and could also experience flooding. CBS News national weather correspondent Rob Maricano has the latest from Illinois.

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur rolls through the Gulf
items.standfirst: Severe weather threatened more than 125 million Americans on Wednesday. Multiple states along the Gulf Coast issued flood alerts as Tropical Storm Arthur rolled through. Rob Marciano has the forecast.
items.content: Severe weather threatened more than 125 million Americans on Wednesday. Multiple states along the Gulf Coast issued flood alerts as Tropical Storm Arthur rolled through. Rob Marciano has the forecast.

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur is the first named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season

clusters.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
clusters.neutral_headline: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
bullet_summary: Reported by Ars Technica News, Wired

items.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
items.standfirst: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.
items.content: Late last week, Anthropic took its new Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models offline following a United States government export-control directive barring “any foreign national” from using the services. The company has been in talks with the White House since Friday but has yet to secure an agreement that would allow it to reinstate the offerings. Since Mythos debuted in April, Anthropic has claimed—and warned—that the model has advanced capabilities for not only finding software vulnerabilities to help defenders patch them, but also figuring out ways to exploit them that could be used by bad actors. Anthropic itself noted this double-edged sword in its launch of Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5. “A great deal of advanced usage of AI models is dual use: the same queries that are beneficial in the hands of cybersecurity professionals and biology researchers could be dangerous if available to malicious actors,” the company wrote in a blog post last week. With this in mind, the company initially released a version called Mythos Preview to a select consortium as part of a working group known as Project Glasswing. Mythos 5 was also privately released to this group last week, while Claude Fable 5, which is a Mythos-grade model, was released to the general public with specific blocks on its ability to give responses to questions about biology and cybersecurity.Read full article Comments

items.title: ‘Dangerous’ AI Models Are Coming No Matter What
items.standfirst: The US government crackdown on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 hides a glaring truth: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.
items.content: The US government crackdown on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 hides a glaring truth: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.
